source_paper_title: An Stochastic Differential Equation Perspective on Stochastic Convex Optimization
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Rodrigo_2025, title={An Stochastic Differential Equation Perspective on Stochastic Convex Optimization}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0162}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0162}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Rodrigo, Maulén S. and Fadili, Jalal and Attouch, Hedy}, year={2025}, month=nov, pages={3190–3221} }
context:
Page 23, Section 4.2 (Remark 4.10 and Conjecture 4.11).

To the best of our knowledge, there is no way to arithmetically solve this non linear ODE, not even a sharp bound of the solution. Nevertheless, if \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\), then \(p_\delta(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\). Which leads us to make the following conjecture: Conjecture 4.11. If \(p_\delta=O(\sigma_\infty^2)\) and \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\) (for constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\)), then \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\).
title: Prove sharp decay rates for nonlinear comparison ODE driven by SDE noise
background_latex:
Let \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) be convex and continuously differentiable with \(L\)-Lipschitz gradient, and assume the minimizer set \(S=\operatorname{argmin} f\neq\emptyset\). Consider the stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX(t)=-\nabla f(X(t))\,dt+\sigma(t,X(t))\,dW(t),\qquad t\ge 0,
\]
where \(W\) is an \(m\)-dimensional Brownian motion and \(\sigma:\mathbb{R}_+\times\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^{d\times m}\) is measurable, globally bounded in Frobenius norm, and Lipschitz in \(x\) uniformly in \(t\). Define the worst-case diffusion magnitude
\[
\sigma_\infty(t):=\sup_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\|\sigma(t,x)\|_F.
\]
Assume \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2(\mathbb{R}_+)\), so the noise vanishes in a square-integrable way.

Assume further that \(f\) satisfies a local error-bound / \L ojasiewicz-type condition near \(S\), leading (after localization arguments) to differential inequalities for certain nonnegative quantities \(y(t)\) (e.g., \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[f(X(t)) - \min f]\) restricted to high-probability events, or \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{dist}(X(t),S)^2/2]\) similarly restricted).

In the analysis, one is led to study a nonlinear scalar ODE (comparison equation)
\[
 y'(t)=-a\,y(t)^b+p_\delta(t),\qquad t>\hat t_\delta,
\]
with parameters \(a>0\), \(b>1\), an initial condition \(y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0\), and a nonnegative forcing term \(p_\delta(t)\) depending on the noise profile. The forcing term is of the same order as \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2\) (more precisely, \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\) in the regime of interest), but it may also depend on \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) through the localization step (Egorov-type arguments).
problem_definition_latex:
Assume \(a>0\), \(b>1\), and let \(p_\delta:[\hat t_\delta,\infty)\to\mathbb{R}_+\) be a measurable function satisfying \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\). Assume also that
\[
\sigma_\infty(t)^2 = O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty),
\]
with constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\). Consider the ODE
\[
 y'(t)=-a\,y(t)^b+p_\delta(t),\qquad t>\hat t_\delta,\qquad y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0.
\]
Establish that the corresponding solution satisfies the decay rate
\[
 y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty).
\]
(Equivalently: prove the stated implication between the forcing decay \(p_\delta(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)}\) and the solution decay \(y(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)}\), uniformly with respect to the localization parameters \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) as above.)
latex:
Let \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) be convex and continuously differentiable with \(L\)-Lipschitz gradient, and assume the minimizer set \(S=\operatorname{argmin} f\neq\emptyset\). Consider the stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX(t)=-\nabla f(X(t))\,dt+\sigma(t,X(t))\,dW(t),\qquad t\ge 0,
\]
where \(W\) is an \(m\)-dimensional Brownian motion and \(\sigma:\mathbb{R}_+\times\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^{d\times m}\) is measurable, globally bounded in Frobenius norm, and Lipschitz in \(x\) uniformly in \(t\). Define the worst-case diffusion magnitude
\[
\sigma_\infty(t):=\sup_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\|\sigma(t,x)\|_F.
\]
Assume \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2(\mathbb{R}_+)\), so the noise vanishes in a square-integrable way.

Assume further that \(f\) satisfies a local error-bound / \L ojasiewicz-type condition near \(S\), leading (after localization arguments) to differential inequalities for certain nonnegative quantities \(y(t)\) (e.g., \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[f(X(t)) - \min f]\) restricted to high-probability events, or \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{dist}(X(t),S)^2/2]\) similarly restricted).

In the analysis, one is led to study a nonlinear scalar ODE (comparison equation)
\[
 y'(t)=-a\,y(t)^b+p_\delta(t),\qquad t>\hat t_\delta,
\]
with parameters \(a>0\), \(b>1\), an initial condition \(y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0\), and a nonnegative forcing term \(p_\delta(t)\) depending on the noise profile. The forcing term is of the same order as \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2\) (more precisely, \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\) in the regime of interest), but it may also depend on \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) through the localization step (Egorov-type arguments).

Assume \(a>0\), \(b>1\), and let \(p_\delta:[\hat t_\delta,\infty)\to\mathbb{R}_+\) be a measurable function satisfying \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\). Assume also that
\[
\sigma_\infty(t)^2 = O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty),
\]
with constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\). Consider the ODE
\[
 y'(t)=-a\,y(t)^b+p_\delta(t),\qquad t>\hat t_\delta,\qquad y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0.
\]
Establish that the corresponding solution satisfies the decay rate
\[
 y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty).
\]
(Equivalently: prove the stated implication between the forcing decay \(p_\delta(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)}\) and the solution decay \(y(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)}\), uniformly with respect to the localization parameters \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) as above.)
open_problem_quote: To the best of our knowledge, there is no way to arithmetically solve this non linear ODE, not even a sharp bound of the solution. Nevertheless, if \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\), then \(p_\delta(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\). Which leads us to make the following conjecture: Conjecture 4.11. If \(p_\delta=O(\sigma_\infty^2)\) and \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\) (for constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\)), then \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The statement is an asymptotic implication for all forcing terms within a big-O class, so finite-dimensional counterexample search is not directly applicable without discretizing an infinite function space and specifying uniformity precisely.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can numerically explore families of forcings \(p_\delta\) and simulate the ODE to test candidate rate bounds and identify worst-case behaviors, but translating numerical evidence into a rigorous uniform bound (especially with dependence on \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\)) remains nontrivial.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In Maul\'en--Fadili--Attouch ("An SDE Perspective on Stochastic Convex Optimization"), the local \L ojasiewicz/error-bound analysis (Theorem 4.5) reduces, after Egorov-type localization on events \(\Omega_\delta\) and comparison (Lemma A.2), to bounding solutions of a nonlinear scalar comparison equation \(y'(t)=-a y(t)^b+p_\delta(t)\) with \(b>1\) and forcing \(p_\delta(t)\) of the same order as \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2\). The paper formulates Conjecture 4.11: if \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\) (uniformly in the localization parameters) and \(p_\delta=O(\sigma_\infty^2)\), then the comparison solution should satisfy the sharp decay \(y(t)=O((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)})\). The difficulty is that the localization time \(\hat t_\delta\) depends on \(\delta\), and the forcing term inherits a nontrivial dependence on \(\delta\) through tail integrals such as \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2 / \sqrt[2q]{\int_{\hat t_\delta}^t \sigma_\infty(u)^2 du}\), so standard explicit ODE solutions do not directly yield uniform-in-\(\delta\) bounds.

Among forward citations, several works develop closely related dissipative-plus-noise differential inequalities and rate balances under error bounds or \L ojasiewicz/KL conditions, but none isolates and proves the conjectured sharp comparison principle in the generality needed here. "Stochastic inertial dynamics via time scaling and averaging" and the two Tikhonov-regularization papers for stochastic (sub)gradient flows in Hilbert spaces obtain explicit polynomial rates by balancing nonlinear dissipation against noise decay \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O(t^{-\alpha})\), providing techniques for tracking how diffusion tails enter remainders. "Continuous-time Analysis of a Stochastic ADMM Method for Nonconvex Composite Optimization" uses a comparison-lemma mechanism in a KL setting to derive exponential/polynomial rates with residual terms controlled by diffusion-energy tails \(U_t\), illustrating how additional assumptions on the forcing (e.g. \(\mathbb E[U_t]\le C/(t+1)^a\)) lead to explicit decay. Related-tool papers on stochastic heavy-ball dynamics and multiscale stochastic inclusions provide Lyapunov/martingale methods and tail-integral controls for vanishing diffusion, but focus mainly on ergodic or accelerated regimes rather than the sharp non-ergodic polynomial decay in Conjecture 4.11.

Overall, the open problem remains unsolved in the forward-citation set: a sharp, uniform (in \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\)) comparison-ODE lemma that converts forcing \(p_\delta(t)=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\) into solution decay \(y(t)=O((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)})\) would close the gap in the local \L ojasiewicz-rate theory for vanishing-noise SDE optimization. Promising directions suggested by the citing literature include: (i) refining nonlinear Gronwall/Bihari-type inequalities with explicit polynomial weights to handle forcing terms involving tail integrals; (ii) constructing explicit supersolutions of the form \(C(t+1)^{-1/(b-1)}\) with carefully chosen \(C\) that absorb the \(\delta\)-dependent prefactors; and (iii) leveraging regular variation/Karamata-type arguments to control expressions like \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2 / (\int_{\hat t_\delta}^t \sigma_\infty^2)^{1/(2q)}\) uniformly over \(\hat t_\delta\).

sources.title: Stochastic inertial dynamics via time scaling and averaging
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves convergence and rate results for stochastic inertial dynamics (S−ISIHD)/(S−ISIHDNS) by time-scaling/averaging reduction to a first-order stochastic (sub)gradient flow. In particular, under 
- convex smooth 
- 
\(\gamma(t)=\alpha/t\) with \(\alpha>3\), \(\beta(t)=t^{\alpha-1}\), and 
- noise integrability \(t\mapsto t^2\sigma_\infty(t)\in L^2([t_0,\infty))\),
the trajectory satisfies \(\mathbb E[f(X(t)) - \min f]=O(t^{-2})\) and \(f(X(t)) - \min f=o(t^{-2})\) a.s. (Corollary 3.5). They also give a local Polyak-

\L ojasiewicz bound yielding an exponential term plus a noise-dependent remainder (Theorem 3.7).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for a sharp comparison-ODE decay: if \(y'(t)=-a y(t)^b + p(t)\) with \(b>1\) and forcing \(p(t)=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\), then \(y(t)=O((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)})\), uniformly over localization parameters. This paper does not analyze that scalar ODE directly. However, its local Polyak-

\L ojasiewicz analysis (Theorem 3.7) effectively controls a localized Lyapunov quantity by a differential inequality of the same general form (dissipative term plus noise term), and it derives explicit rates/remainders in terms of \(\sigma_\infty\) (through functions like \(l_\delta\) built from \(\sigma_\infty^2\) and its tail integral). Thus it provides partial progress/techniques toward handling the forcing-vs-decay interplay, but it does not establish the specific sharp polynomial implication \(p(t)=O(t^{-b/(b-1)})\Rightarrow y(t)=O(t^{-1/(b-1)})\) for the comparison ODE, nor the requested uniformity in \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\).

sources.title: Tikhonov regularization for stochastic non-smooth convex optimization in Hilbert spaces
sources.theorem_or_result:
In the smooth case with Tikhonov regularization, the paper proves explicit expectation rates for the stochastic flow
\[dX(t)=-\nabla f(X(t))dt- t^{-r}X(t)dt+\sigma(t,X(t))dW(t),\]
under a local Hölderian error bound \(f\in EB_p(S)\) and square-integrable, nonincreasing noise \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2\). In particular (Theorem 4.15), if \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O(t^{-\alpha})\) with \(\alpha>1\), then
- \(\mathbb E[f(X(t)) - \min f]=O(t^{-r}+R(t))\) with \(R(t)=O(t^{r-\alpha})\), hence \(\mathbb E[f(X(t)) - \min f]=O(t^{-\min\{r,\alpha-r\}})\).
They also show a.s. weak convergence for SDIs with \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2\) (Theorem 3.6) and a.s. strong convergence to the minimum-norm minimizer for SDI-TA under conditions (T1)–(T3) on \(\varepsilon(t)\) (Theorem 4.1), with an explicit tuning \(\varepsilon(t)=t^{-r}\), \(r>\frac{2p}{2p+1}\), when \(F\in EB_p(S_F)\) (Theorem 4.11).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a sharp comparison-ODE decay: if \(y'=-ay^b+p(t)\) with \(b>1\) and forcing \(p(t)=O(t^{-b/(b-1)})\), then \(y(t)=O(t^{-1/(b-1)})\), uniformly over localization parameters. This paper does not directly analyze that scalar ODE or prove the stated sharp implication. However, it develops closely related rate machinery for stochastic (sub)gradient flows under local error bounds/Łojasiewicz conditions and vanishing noise, yielding polynomial decay rates in expectation that arise from differential/energy inequalities of the same general type (dissipative term plus noise forcing). In particular, their rate statements (e.g., Theorem 4.15) effectively balance a nonlinear dissipation coming from an error bound with a forcing term controlled by \(\sigma_\infty^2\), but the exponents and the comparison argument are embedded in a different Lyapunov framework (with Tikhonov regularization) rather than the specific ODE \(y'=-ay^b+p\). Thus it provides partial progress/related evidence but not a direct solution of the open ODE comparison problem in full generality or with the requested uniformity in \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\).

sources.title: Stochastic differential inclusions and Tikhonov regularization for stochastic non-smooth convex optimization in Hilbert spaces
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves convergence and explicit rates for stochastic subgradient/gradient flows (and Tikhonov-regularized variants) under vanishing diffusion 

- For (SDI) with 
\(dX(t)\in-\partial(f+g)(X(t))dt+\sigma(t,X(t))dW(t)\) and \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2\), Theorem 3.6 gives a.s. weak convergence to \(\operatorname{argmin}(f+g)\) and \(\int_{t_0}^\infty (F(X(t)) - \min F)dt<\infty\) (under continuity of \(g\)).

- For the smooth Tikhonov-regularized SDE 
\(dX(t)=-\nabla f(X(t))dt-t^{-r}X(t)dt+\sigma(t,X(t))dW(t)\), with \(f\in EB_p(S)\) and \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O(t^{-\alpha})\), Theorem 4.15 yields \(\mathbb E[f(X(t)) - \min f]=O(t^{-r}+R(t))\) with \(R(t)=O(t^{r-\alpha})\) (hence \(\mathbb E[f(X(t)) - \min f]=O(t^{-\min\{r,\alpha-r\}})\) when \(\alpha>1\)), and \(\mathbb E\|X(t)-x^\star\|^2=O(t^{-(1-r)}+t^{-r/p}+t^{-(\alpha-2r)})\) under stated parameter ranges.
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for a sharp comparison-ODE estimate: if \(y'(t)=-a y(t)^b+p(t)\) with \(b>1\) and forcing \(p(t)=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\), then \(y(t)=O((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)})\), uniformly over localization parameters.

This paper does not directly analyze that scalar ODE or prove the stated implication in that generality. However, it addresses the same SDE/optimization setting (vanishing diffusion \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2\), local error bounds/Łojasiewicz via \(EB_p\)) and derives explicit polynomial decay rates for objective gaps and distances in expectation (Theorem 4.15) under assumptions of the form \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O(t^{-\alpha})\). These rates are obtained via Lyapunov/Itô analysis rather than a sharp nonlinear comparison-ODE lemma, and the exponents differ because the dynamics includes an additional Tikhonov term \(t^{-r}X(t)\) and focuses on \(EB_p\) rather than a generic \(y^b\) dissipation.

Thus it provides partial progress/related evidence on how noise decay translates into solution/value decay, but it does not settle the specific sharp ODE comparison statement requested.

sources.title: Continuous-time Analysis of a Stochastic ADMM Method for Nonconvex Composite Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves convergence and convergence-rate bounds for the SDE model of stochastic proximal linearized ADMM:
- For the SDE 
\[dx(t)=-(1/\lambda)\nabla H_\mu(x(t))\,dt+(1/(\lambda\sqrt{\rho}))\,\sigma(x(t))\,dW(t),\]
with bounded Lipschitz diffusion \(\|\sigma(x)\|_F\le \bar\sigma\) and \(U_t:=\int_t^\infty \|\sigma(x(s))\|_F^2 ds\), if \(\mathbb E[U_0]<\infty\) then \(H_\mu(x(t))\) converges a.s. and \(\|\nabla H_\mu(x(t))\|\to 0\) a.s. (Theorem 2).
- Under a (uniformized) Lojasiewicz inequality with exponent \(\theta\in(0,1)\) and boundedness of \(x(t)\), they obtain in-expectation rates for the objective gap:
  - if \(\theta\in(0,1/2]\): \(\mathbb E|H_\mu(x(t))-H_{\mu,\infty}|\le a_1 e^{-b_1(t-T)}+c_1/\rho\);
  - if \(\theta\in(1/2,1)\): \(\mathbb E|H_\mu(x(t))-H_{\mu,\infty}|\le a_2 (t-T)^{1/(1-2\theta)}+c_2/\rho\) (Theorem 3).
- If additionally \(\mathbb E[U_t]\le C/(t+1)^a\) with \(a>1\), then \(x(t)\) converges a.s. to a critical point of \(H_\mu\) and they derive rates for \(\mathbb E\|x(t)-x_\infty\|\) (Theorems 4–5).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks for sharp polynomial decay of solutions to a scalar comparison ODE \(y'(t)=-a y(t)^b+p(t)\) when \(p(t)=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\), namely \(y(t)=O((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)})\), uniformly over localization parameters.

This paper does not analyze that scalar ODE directly, nor does it prove the stated sharp implication for general measurable \(p_\delta\). However, it uses essentially the same comparison-ODE mechanism (their Lemma 5) to control a Lyapunov gap \(L(t)=\mathbb E[L(x(t))]-\bar L\) via an inequality of the form
\[L(t)\le L(T)-b\int_T^t L(s)^{2\theta}\,ds + c(t-T),\]
which corresponds to the ODE template with exponent \(b=2\theta\) and forcing \(\approx c\) (or with additional forcing coming from \(\int E[U_s]^{2\theta} ds\)). They obtain explicit decay bounds (exponential when \(2\theta\le 1\), polynomial when \(2\theta>1\)) under stronger assumptions such as \(\mathbb E[U_t]\le C/(t+1)^a\) with \(a>1\).

Thus the paper provides related comparison-lemma technology and rate derivations in an SDE/KL setting, but it does not establish the specific sharp polynomial rate \((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)}\) from forcing \((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)}\) for the nonlinear ODE in the open problem, nor the uniformity in \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\).

sources.title: Long-Time Analysis of Stochastic Heavy Ball Dynamics for Convex Optimization and Monotone Equations
sources.theorem_or_result:
For stochastic Heavy Ball dynamics with friction (SHBF) in a Hilbert space,
- dY(t)=X(t)dt,
- dX(t)=(-\lambda X(t)-b(t)\nabla f(Y(t)))dt+\sigma_Y(t)dW(t),
assuming \(\int_{t_0}^\infty \|\sigma_Y(t)\|_{HS}^2dt<\infty\) and \(\sup_{t\ge t_0} \dot b(t)/b(t)<\lambda\), the authors prove almost sure rates \(f(Y(t)) - \inf f = o(1/b(t))\) and \(\|X(t)\|=o(1)\), and expected rate \(\mathbb E[f(Y(t)) - \inf f]=O(1/b(t))\) (Theorem 2.2). Under an additional integrability condition \(\int_{t_0}^\infty \frac{1}{b(s)}(\int_{(s+t_0)/2}^s b(r)dr)\|\sigma_Y(s)\|_{HS}^2ds<\infty\), they obtain improved a.s. bounds
\[f(Y(t)) - \inf f = O\Big(\frac{1}{\int_{(t+t_0)/2}^t b(s)ds}\Big),\qquad \|X(t)\| = O\Big(\sqrt{\frac{b(t)}{\int_{(t+t_0)/2}^t b(s)ds}}\Big).\]
They also transfer these results via time-rescaling to stochastic Su–Boyd–Cand\`es (SAVD), yielding \(f(Z(s)) - \inf f = o(1/s^2)\) a.s. and \(\mathbb E[f(Z(s)) - \inf f]=O(1/s^2)\) under \(\int s^2\|\sigma_Z(s)\|_{HS}^2ds<\infty\) (Theorem 2.7).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp polynomial decay for solutions of a scalar comparison ODE \(y'(t)=-a y(t)^b+p_\delta(t)\) when \(p_\delta(t)\) decays like \((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)}\), uniformly in localization parameters. This paper does not study that scalar nonlinear ODE nor prove a general comparison lemma of the requested type. Instead it derives convergence rates for stochastic second-order optimization/monotone-operator dynamics using Lyapunov/energy estimates and martingale convergence (Mao’s theorem), under square-integrability (and sometimes stronger weighted integrability) assumptions on the diffusion. These techniques are related in spirit to controlling forcing/noise terms in differential inequalities, but they do not establish the specific implication \(p(t)=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\Rightarrow y(t)=O((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)})\) for the nonlinear comparison ODE, nor address uniformity in \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\).

sources.title: Asymptotic behaviour of coupled random dynamical systems with multiscale aspects
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper studies stochastic differential inclusions of the form 
\[dX(t)+A(X(t))\,dt+\beta(t)\nabla\Psi(X(t))\,dt\ni\sigma(t,X(t))\,dW(t),\]
with maximally monotone drift part and vanishing diffusion magnitude \(\Sigma(t)=\sup_x\|\sigma(t,x)\|_F\in L^2(0,\infty)\). Under the Attouch–Czarnecki integrability condition \(\int_0^\infty \beta(t)\big(\Psi^*(p/\beta(t)) - s_C(p/\beta(t))\big)dt<\infty\) and standard Lipschitz assumptions on \(\sigma\), they prove almost sure asymptotic feasibility \(\Psi(X(t))\to 0\) and convergence of objective values in the potential case \(A=\partial\Phi\): \(\Phi(X(t))\to \min_C\Phi\) a.s. They also establish finite-time ergodic rates, e.g. for the Cesàro average \(\bar X(t)=t^{-1}\int_0^t X(s)ds\), 
\[\mathbb E[\Theta_\delta(\bar X(t))]=O(1/t),\qquad \mathbb E[\Phi(\bar X(t)) - \min_C\Phi]\le \frac{\mathrm{dist}(x,S)^2}{2t}+\frac{1}{2t}\int_0^t \Sigma(s)^2 ds.\]
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp polynomial decay of solutions to a scalar comparison ODE \(y'=-a y^b + p_\delta(t)\) when the forcing satisfies \(p_\delta(t)=O((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)})\), uniformly in localization parameters. This paper does not analyze that nonlinear comparison ODE nor prove the specific implication \(p(t)=O(t^{-b/(b-1)})\Rightarrow y(t)=O(t^{-1/(b-1)})\). Instead, it develops convergence and rate bounds for stochastic differential inclusions with vanishing noise \(\Sigma\in L^2\), yielding mainly ergodic \(O(1/t)\) rates for gap functions / objective gaps and almost sure convergence of values. These results could serve as background/tools for SDE-based optimization analyses, but they do not address the sharp non-ergodic polynomial decay regime governed by a \L ojasiewicz exponent \(b>1\) and a forcing term of order \(\Sigma(t)^2\).
